description: Tapestry is a New York-based fashion and accessories group focused on Coach and Kate Spade New York, with Coach carrying most of the business while Kate Spade works through a polished turnaround.

overview.title: Business Profile
paragraphs: Tapestry is a global house of fashion and lifestyle brands built around Coach and Kate Spade New York. Its products include handbags, accessories, footwear, apparel, gifts, home goods, and licensed brand extensions sold through owned stores, e-commerce, wholesale, and licensing channels.

The portfolio is now leaner than older descriptions suggest: Tapestry completed the sale of Stuart Weitzman to Caleres in August 2025. Coach remains the main revenue engine, while Kate Spade is the smaller lifestyle brand being reset, which makes the holding-company story fairly simple once the brand poetry is folded away.

overview.title: Registry Fit
paragraphs: For Free The World, Tapestry fits cleanly under consumer-discretionary and apparel-retail. The most relevant product mapping should start with Coach and Kate Spade New York, then break down into handbags, leather goods, fashion accessories, apparel, footwear, jewelry, home decor, and licensed fragrance or eyewear where source support is available.

Likely alternative paths are not direct software replacements but consumer substitutes: secondhand and resale markets, repair services, local makers, cooperative or independent fashion labels, open design patterns, circular materials, and durable goods that do not need a quarterly campaign to justify existing.

overview.title: Operating Model
paragraphs: Tapestry depends on brand equity, retail merchandising, store experience, digital commerce, global sourcing, and disciplined pricing. Coach's fiscal 2025 performance made it the dominant segment, while Kate Spade's decline and impairment charges make brand recovery a central research angle.

Supply-chain transparency is especially important for this company. Tapestry publishes responsibility reports and Tier 1 supplier disclosures, useful for tracing the factories, materials, labor standards, and climate claims behind the handbags.

overview.title: Technology Posture
paragraphs: No technology wave is currently mapped in the repo context, and none should be attached solely because Tapestry sells through modern retail channels. That said, the company has highlighted digital expertise, omni-channel commerce, and proprietary data systems as part of its operating model.

Tapestry's 2026 patent announcement for Mira, an internal AI platform connected to its Global Data Fabric, is worth flagging for future review. It appears to support assortment planning, inventory management, and consumer-trend analysis rather than a consumer-facing product category.

sourceSuggestions.title: Tapestry Company Overview
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Official overview of corporate identity, leadership, and strategy language.

sourceSuggestions.title: Tapestry Our Brands
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Best official source for current brand roster, brand positioning, store counts, and brand-level sales highlights.

sourceSuggestions.title: Tapestry Investor Relations
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Primary gateway for financial reports, filings, presentations, and investor updates.

sourceSuggestions.title: Tapestry FY2025 Form 10-K Mirror

sourceSuggestions.title: Tapestry FY2025 Corporate Responsibility Report
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Supports research on climate, materials, labor, sourcing, and responsibility claims.

sourceSuggestions.title: Tapestry Supplier List
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Direct source for Tier 1 supplier disclosure and supply-chain transparency commitments.

sourceSuggestions.title: Tapestry Mira AI Platform Announcement
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Documents the company's internal AI and data platform claims for possible future technology-wave review.

sourceSuggestions.title: AP News on Stuart Weitzman Sale
sourceSuggestions.whyItMatters: Independent confirmation of the Stuart Weitzman sale and the renewed focus on Coach and Kate Spade.
